0:
I remember my father's words.
How they rang in my ears as we left Castrum Abania behind and looked out over the lochs before us.

I remember my father's words.
How they rang in my ears as we left Castrum Abania behind and looked out over the lochs before us.

1:
Comrades brave and true. They had come from all across Eorzea to stand with us.
To stand against oppression and tyranny.

Comrades brave and true. They had come from all across Eorzea to stand with us.
To stand against oppression and tyranny.

2:
Twenty years the Empire had ruled us, but no longer.
Liberty or death. Liberty or death.

Twenty years the Empire had ruled us, but no longer.
Liberty or death. Liberty or death.

7:
A long siege wouldn't be good for the people in the city either.
If food becomes scarce, you know who'll starve first.

A long siege wouldn't be good for the people in the city either.
If food becomes scarce, you know who'll starve first.

8:
Just so. Therefore, I propose we commit our forces to an all-out assault.
What say you?

Just so. Therefore, I propose we commit our forces to an all-out assault.
What say you?

20:
Rhalgr's Beacon nears its end.
Soon, my friends, Ala Mhigo will be ours once more!

Rhalgr's Beacon nears its end.
Soon, my friends, Ala Mhigo will be ours once more!

26:
Conrad, Meffrid, your own comrades!
I should kill you here and now for what you did!

Conrad, Meffrid, your own comrades!
I should kill you here and now for what you did!

41:
...No. You're gonna live long enough to see us win our freedom.
I promise you that.

...No. You're gonna live long enough to see us win our freedom.
I promise you that.

48:
None. The search goes on in earnest, but Cid has thus far found nothing of note. Mayhap the Garleans beat us to it. If they were working desperately
to repair the machine, that might explain their decision to remain within
their walls...

None. The search goes on in earnest, but Cid has thus far found nothing of note. Mayhap the Garleans beat us to it. If they were working desperately
to repair the machine, that might explain their decision to remain within
their walls...

49:
Ah, Lyse. I trust you surrendered Fordola into custody without incident.
We were just discussing the pilus's claims.

Ah, Lyse. I trust you surrendered Fordola into custody without incident.
We were just discussing the pilus's claims.

53:
Yes... It is a place wholly dedicated to the creation of enhanced beings whom they refer to as “the Resonant.” As I understand it, the process aims to
imbue a test subject with the qualities of an individual with demonstrated
magical aptitude.
This entails... This entails the m-mechanical extraction and transfusion of some manner of...primordial essence.

Yes... It is a place wholly dedicated to the creation of enhanced beings whom they refer to as “the Resonant.” As I understand it, the process aims to
imbue a test subject with the qualities of an individual with demonstrated
magical aptitude.
This entails... This entails the m-mechanical extraction and transfusion of some manner of...primordial essence.

54:
So that's how Fordola improved so fast. I knew there had to be something.
She wasn't half as strong or quick to predict my attacks when we first met.

So that's how Fordola improved so fast. I knew there had to be something.
She wasn't half as strong or quick to predict my attacks when we first met.

55:
If it weren't for Urianger's siphon thingy, I really don't think we'd have
beaten her...

If it weren't for Urianger's siphon thingy, I really don't think we'd have
beaten her...

59:
A most impressive feat under any circumstances, but an almost miraculous
one when considered in the context of Fordola's unfamiliarity with my
lady's arts. How then could she so perfectly predict an unknown opponent's
every move?

A most impressive feat under any circumstances, but an almost miraculous
one when considered in the context of Fordola's unfamiliarity with my
lady's arts. How then could she so perfectly predict an unknown opponent's
every move?

63:
I would fashion a device with the capacity to generate a massive disruption
in the surrounding aether. If, as I suspected, Fordola's senses were
indeed questing for the most minor of oscillations, the shock of exposure
to a more amplitudinous aetheric waveform would be akin to a debilitating
bout of aether sickness.

I would fashion a device with the capacity to generate a massive disruption
in the surrounding aether. If, as I suspected, Fordola's senses were
indeed questing for the most minor of oscillations, the shock of exposure
to a more amplitudinous aetheric waveform would be akin to a debilitating
bout of aether sickness.

65:
Indeed so, Master Alphinaud.
All things considered, 'twas a most fortuitous outcome.

Indeed so, Master Alphinaud.
All things considered, 'twas a most fortuitous outcome.

73:
Exactly! Ohhh, I wouldn't want to be in Zenos's boots!
Not with the likes of us coming to get him!

Exactly! Ohhh, I wouldn't want to be in Zenos's boots!
Not with the likes of us coming to get him!

74:
Well, no one's saying we shouldn't be cautious!
...Though maybe I was getting a bit carried away.

Well, no one's saying we shouldn't be cautious!
...Though maybe I was getting a bit carried away.

75:
Enough talking! All right, all right! I get the message!
Action is what's needed now!

Enough talking! All right, all right! I get the message!
Action is what's needed now!

90:
Bear witness, my Eorzean brothers and sisters!
Doma is come to pay her debts!

Bear witness, my Eorzean brothers and sisters!
Doma is come to pay her debts!

92:
We will deal with the flying machines!
Forward, my friends! For freedom and justice!

We will deal with the flying machines!
Forward, my friends! For freedom and justice!

124:
I know you understand this. You and I are one and the same.
Together, we could while away the quiet hours, as friend and confidant...if you will accept me.

I know you understand this. You and I are one and the same.
Together, we could while away the quiet hours, as friend and confidant...if you will accept me.

131:
I live for them too! This is who we are, my friend! This is all we are!
Ala Mhigo and Doma and Garlemald be damned!

I live for them too! This is who we are, my friend! This is all we are!
Ala Mhigo and Doma and Garlemald be damned!

139:
And yet...there is only joy. Transcendent joy that I have never known.
How invigorating, how...pure, this feeling.

And yet...there is only joy. Transcendent joy that I have never known.
How invigorating, how...pure, this feeling.

140:
Is that what this was all about? All the meaningless death and destruction?
So you could feel something!?

Is that what this was all about? All the meaningless death and destruction?
So you could feel something!?

141:
Meaningless? Men die that others may live.
Those who survive are stronger for it.

Meaningless? Men die that others may live.
Those who survive are stronger for it.

158:
O come ye wayward brothers,
Bereft of hearth and home,

O come ye wayward brothers,
Bereft of hearth and home,

159:
Beneath yon burning star there lies
A haven for the bold.

Beneath yon burning star there lies
A haven for the bold.

160:
Raise up your hands and voices,
Let fill your hearts with pride,

Raise up your hands and voices,
Let fill your hearts with pride,

161:
Above the churning waters we
Stand strong and unified.

Above the churning waters we
Stand strong and unified.

162:
In crimson it began, and in crimson it ended.
And then...and then we were free.

In crimson it began, and in crimson it ended.
And then...and then we were free.

166:
Even Zenos was once a baby in the cradle, innocent and pure.
He was made into a creature that lived only for death, not born one.

Even Zenos was once a baby in the cradle, innocent and pure.
He was made into a creature that lived only for death, not born one.

168:
It won't be easy to heal a generation's worth of wounds, to bridge the gap.
But I won't give up, Papalymo. I won't.

But I won't give up, Papalymo. I won't.

183:
Now, I'm obviously a Scion, but as Conrad left me in charge of things here,
I also have responsibilities to Ala Mhigo...

189:
But this I say with the utmost sincerity: Scion or no, we are comrades.
Now and ever after.

Now and ever after.

To think fishing could be so difficult!
